name: API-s-for-OSINT
summary:
APIs For OSINT

This is a Collection of APIs that will be useful for automating various tasks in OSINT.

APIs For OSINT

This is a Collection of APIs that will be useful for automating various tasks in OSINT.

function_signatures.description: Index entry in the OSINT category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_osint_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the OSINT system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for API-s-for-OSINT (cipher387/API-s-for-OSINT). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for API-s-for-OSINT. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with cipher387/osint_stuff_tool_collection (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with wso2/phuzz (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with utkusen/urlhunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the OSINT category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the OSINT system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
